MEDB52StrengthAssessmentNASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for muscle strength assessment. Captures isokinetic and isometric strength data for spaceflight crew members.Questionnairehttps://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/Questionnaire/medb-5-2-strength-assessment#0.5.9
overviewMedical Requirements Overview0..1group
overview.mrid-numberMRID Number and Title0..1displayInitial Value: string = MEDB 5.2 Muscle Strength Assessment

Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Sponsor Organizations#exercise-physiology "Exercise Physiology"
overview.disciplineDiscipline1..1choiceOptions: 1 option
Initial Value: Coding = NASA MRID Medical Disciplines#bone-muscle-exercise "Bone/Muscle/Exercise"
overview.purposePurpose/Objectives1..1textInitial Value: string = To assess muscle strength using isokinetic dynamometry. Establish baseline and monitor for spaceflight-related muscle atrophy and strength loss.
overview.measurement-parametersMeasurement Parameters1..1stringInitial Value: string = Peak torque, total work, average power for knee, ankle, trunk muscle groups
overview.flight-durationFlight Duration1..1stringInitial Value: string = >= 30 days

preflight-activities.scheduleSchedule0..1stringInitial Value: string = L-180 to L-60 days

Documentation for this format

Options Sets

Answer options for overview.sponsor

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-mrid-sponsor-cs#exercise-physiology ("Exercise Physiology")

Answer options for overview.discipline

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-mrid-discipline-cs#bone-muscle-exercise ("Bone/Muscle/Exercise")

Answer options for postflight-strength-results.muscle-atrophy-assessment

  • Minimal (<5% decline)
  • Mild (5-10% decline)
  • Moderate (10-20% decline)
  • Significant (>20% decline)

Answer options for data-delivery.archive

  • https://mitre.org/fhir/space-health/CodeSystem/nasa-data-archive-cs#mmis ("Medical Mission Information System")

extension.valueString: The astronaut/crewmember subject of this medical requirement

name: MEDB52StrengthAssessment
title: MEDB 5.2 Strength Assessment
description: NASA Medical Requirements Integration Document for muscle strength assessment. Captures isokinetic and isometric strength data for spaceflight crew members.
purpose: To assess pre- and postflight muscle strength using isokinetic and isometric testing to monitor for spaceflight-related muscle atrophy.
copyright: NASA Johnson Space Center - Medical Requirements Integration Documents (MRIDs) JSC28913

initial.valueString: To assess muscle strength using isokinetic dynamometry. Establish baseline and monitor for spaceflight-related muscle atrophy and strength loss.
Peak torque, total work, average power for knee, ankle, trunk muscle groups
Isokinetic strength testing of major muscle groups including knee extensors/flexors, ankle plantar/dorsiflexors, and trunk extensors/flexors.
R+5 to R+7 days, R+30 days
